steps.title: Identify the role of the natural observation
steps.content: The underlined portion presents wind shaking seed pods—a concrete event from the natural world. What follows shows the speaker using this image as a metaphor for interior turbulence: thoughts are “spent as the black seeds,” and the speaker is “scattered like the hot shriveled seeds.” The natural event serves as the foundation for expressing emotional disturbance.

steps.title: Match the function to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice A directly captures this: the speaker observes an occurrence in nature and then expands on it to convey turbulent interior state. The other three misidentify or distort this relationship—B creates a non-existent contrast, D imposes a meaning about “commonality” the poem never makes, and C treats the natural event as causally responsible rather than metaphorically parallel. That matches choice A — A is correct.
